title: Decarbonizing Aluminum: Rolling Out a More Sustainable Sector
summary: This article examines how aluminum recycling and decarbonization technologies are reducing the sector’s carbon intensity. It highlights efforts such as Elysis, a Montreal-based venture aiming to eliminate direct GHG emissions from aluminum smelting.

title: ARTICLE: Update on the Aluminum Industry Response to Climate Change
summary: The piece reviews the aluminum industry's response to climate change, focusing on lower-carbon and “green” aluminum trends. It also discusses certification efforts like the Aluminium Stewardship Initiative and the need for pilot projects using renewable power in smelting.

title: 3 technologies helping the aluminium industry decarbonize
summary: This article describes three technologies that could accelerate decarbonization in aluminum production. It includes Hydro’s HalZero process, which aims to eliminate CO2 emissions during electrolysis, and innovations that improve recycling by removing metal impurities.

title: Roadmap to net-zero
summary: Hydro outlines its plan to reduce the carbon footprint of raw materials used in aluminum production and move toward net-zero. The roadmap emphasizes sustainability improvements across the aluminum value chain.

title: HRC Steel Price - Chart - Historical Data - News

title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News

title: Current State of the U.S. Steel Market
summary: The article reviews recent U.S. steel market conditions, noting capacity utilization has slipped below the administration’s 80% goal but has shown signs of stabilizing in recent weeks. title: Industry Data

title: United States Steel Production
summary: Trading Economics reports that U.S. steel production rose to 7.2 million tonnes in March 2026 from 6.5 million tonnes in February 2026. The page provides historical context and tracks production trends over time using World Steel Association data.

title: A streamlined way to make steel could reduce America's reliance on imports
summary: Hertha Metals is scaling a single-step steelmaking system that can produce molten steel from iron ore of any grade using a continuous electric arc furnace. The process replaces coal with natural gas and electricity, and can also run on clean hydrogen, cutting energy use and emissions while aiming to strengthen U.S. steel supply.

title: 3 innovations in green steel
summary: This roundup highlights several advances in low-carbon steelmaking, including hydrogen-based processes, reduced-coke blast furnace techniques, and a large-scale green steel plant under development. It focuses on technologies intended to cut or eliminate CO2 emissions from traditional steel production.

title: Smelting Barriers: The Innovators Forging a New Future for Iron and Steel
summary: RMI examines emerging startups and technologies that are reshaping iron and steel production through electrification, alternative feedstocks, recycling, and novel low-emissions processes. The piece emphasizes that reaching net-zero will require scaling new methods alongside improvements to existing steel assets.

title: Copper Strengthens as Risk Appetite Returns

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: Indonesia has reportedly ordered PT Weda Bay Nickel, the world's biggest nickel mine, to sharply cut production from 42 million tons of ore quota in 2025 to 12 million tons this year. The move is part of Jakarta's effort to support global nickel prices and manage supply more tightly. The company says it plans to seek a revision.

title: Nickel futures recover after reports of additional output reductions
summary: Nickel prices moved back above $18,800 per ton after reports of further supply reductions, extending a recent rebound from a near four-week low. The move reflects market sensitivity to potential production cuts and tightening supply expectations. The article focuses on pricing, but the driver is directly tied to production and capacity news.

title: Indonesia navigates nickel market with output cuts, policy shifts
summary: Indonesia said it plans to cut nickel production in 2026 to support prices and government revenue, while also tightening oversight of mining activity.
